summary.intent: Merge the main branch into the mcp-timeout-config feature branch, resolve all conflicts, then commit and push the result.
summary.outcome: Successfully resolved 5 merge conflicts in page-mcp.js by combining the timeout feature (HEAD) with the display_name feature (origin/main), then committed and pushed the merge.

repo: The mcp-timeout-config branch adds timeout configuration (timeoutVal, editTimeout) to MCP server forms.
The main branch concurrently added display_name support (displayNameVal, editDisplayName, renderServerName) to the same file, causing conflicts.
Both SSE and stdio server add flows exist in page-mcp.js and required parallel conflict resolution.

code.finding: ConfigForm signature conflict: HEAD added timeoutVal parameter; main added renderServerName function. Both changes must coexist.
Signal declarations conflict: HEAD had timeoutVal signal; main added displayNameVal signal. Both signals are needed.
SSE addCustomServer conflict: HEAD used addCustomServer pattern with timeout field; main added display_name field. Resolution keeps addCustomServer pattern and adds display_name.
stdio addCustomServer conflict: same pattern as SSE — keep addCustomServer with timeout and add display_name.
Edit initialization conflict: HEAD had editTimeout; main added editDisplayName. Both init values must be set.

workflow: Reading the full conflicted file multiple times was necessary to understand all 5 conflict sites before resolving any of them.
Resolving conflicts one at a time with individual Edit calls (rather than a full rewrite) is safer and more reviewable.

friction: Required 6+ sequential reads of the same file to map all conflict regions before editing could begin, indicating the file is large and conflicts were spread throughout.
